title: commiphora crenulata
intro: Commiphora crenulata, also known as African myrrh or Commiphora, is a type of shrub or small tree native to Africa and the Middle East, belonging to the family Burseraceae. The resin extracted from Commiphora crenulata has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Commiphora crenulata have been found to have numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. Commiphora crenulata can be used in various herbal preparations, such as essential oils, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically or ingested under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
